skillsEn.plainEn: After damage, take one card from every other character, then turn face down.

skillsEn.textEn: Locked skill: Other characters calculate their distance to you as 1 greater.
skillsEn.plainEn: Everyone else is effectively one distance farther from you.

summaryEn: Divine Lü Bu builds Rage marks by dealing and taking damage, but ordinary trick cards cost Rage or HP. Spend Rage to disable armor, gain Unrivaled, or unleash a devastating whole-field attack.
timingEn: Balance the cost of ordinary tricks against their value, and save six Rage marks for Wrath of the Gods when the mass discard will matter.
exampleEn: You start with 2 Rage marks, then deal 1 damage and gain another. During your Play Phase, you spend 2 Rage to gain Unrivaled and disable an enemy's armor. With 6 Rage, you can spend them to deal every other character 1 damage, make them discard all equipment and four hand cards, then turn face down.
misunderstandingsEn: Frenzy gives 2 Rage at game start and 1 after each point of damage dealt or received.
Ordinary trick cards cost either 1 Rage or 1 HP under No Strategy.
Wrath of the Gods also turns you face down after its damage and discards resolve.

skillsEn.textEn: Locked skill: At the start of the game, you gain 2 Rage marks. After you deal or take 1 damage, you gain 1 Rage mark.
skillsEn.plainEn: Start with two Rage and gain one whenever you deal or take damage.

skillsEn.textEn: Locked skill: When you use an ordinary trick card, you discard one Rage mark or lose 1 HP.
skillsEn.plainEn: Every ordinary trick costs one Rage or 1 HP.

skillsEn.textEn: During your Play Phase, you may discard 2 Rage marks and choose one other character. Until the end of this turn, you gain Unrivaled, and that character's armor is ineffective.
skillsEn.plainEn: Spend two Rage to ignore one enemy's armor and gain Unrivaled for the turn.

skillsEn.textEn: Once during your Play Phase, you may discard 6 Rage marks, then deal 1 damage to each other character. Those characters first discard all cards in their equipment areas, then discard four hand cards, and finally you turn face down.
skillsEn.plainEn: Spend six Rage to damage everyone else, destroy their equipment and four hand cards, then turn face down.

summaryEn: Divine Zhao Yun has a larger hand limit and gains a card when entering or leaving the dying state. Dragon Soul converts up to two cards of one suit into key basic cards and can strengthen red effects or discard an enemy card with black cards.
timingEn: Preserve matching suits for the stronger two-card conversions, while using your large hand limit to maintain both offense and defense.
exampleEn: You enter the dying state and draw 1 card from Last Stand. You use two Hearts cards as Peach, increasing its recovery by 1. Alternatively, two Spades can be used as Negate and then discard one card from the current-turn character.
misunderstandingsEn: Last Stand triggers both when you enter and when you leave the dying state.
Dragon Soul uses up to two cards of the same suit, not necessarily exactly two.
The red bonus applies when two red cards are used; the black bonus applies when two black cards are used.

skillsEn.textEn: Locked skill: Your hand-card limit is increased by 2. When you enter or leave the dying state, you draw one card.
skillsEn.plainEn: Hold two extra cards and draw whenever you enter or leave the dying state.

skillsEn.textEn: You may use or play up to two cards of the same suit according to these rules: Hearts as Peach; Diamonds as Fire Slash; Clubs as Jink; Spades as Negate. If you use two red cards this way, increase this card's recovery value or damage value by 1. If you use two black cards this way, discard one card from the current-turn character.
skillsEn.plainEn: Convert matching-suit cards into Peach, Fire Slash, Jink, or Negate. Two red cards strengthen the effect; two black cards discard a card from the active character.

summaryEn: Divine Sima Yi stores Restraint marks from damage and discards, then awakens to spend them on a toolkit of borrowed skills. Killing a character can grant an extra turn, letting him snowball one target at a time.
timingEn: Build four Restraint marks safely, spend Extreme Strategy only when its specific effect is needed, and weaken several enemies so Chain Break can continue your turn after a kill.
exampleEn: You take 2 damage and gain 2 Restraint marks. After discarding 2 hand cards in Discard Phase, you gain 2 more and awaken at the next Preparation Phase, losing 1 maximum HP and gaining Extreme Strategy. You later kill a character and take an extra turn after the current turn ends.
misunderstandingsEn: Restraint gains marks from both damage and hand cards discarded during Discard Phase.
Awakening requires at least 4 Restraint marks at the start of Preparation Phase.
Chain Break grants the extra turn after the current turn ends, not immediately.

skillsEn.textEn: Locked skill: After you take damage, or discard hand cards during your Discard Phase, you gain X Restraint marks, where X is the damage amount or number of hand cards discarded.
skillsEn.plainEn: Damage and discarded hand cards become Restraint marks.

skillsEn.textEn: Awakening skill: At the start of your Preparation Phase, if you have at least 4 Restraint marks, reduce your maximum HP by 1 and gain Extreme Strategy: You may discard 1 Restraint mark to activate one of these skills: Rebellion, Exile, Ingenuity, Purification, or No Escape.
skillsEn.plainEn: At four marks, awaken and spend marks to access five different skills.

skillsEn.textEn: After you kill any character, you may gain an additional turn after this turn ends.
skillsEn.plainEn: A kill can give you another full turn after the current one.

summaryEn: Reimagined Liu Bei gives hand cards to allies and can treat his second gift to one ally in the phase as a basic card. As lord, he can ask Shu allies to provide Slash through Inspire.
timingEn: Give allies the cards they need, especially defense, and remember that the second card given to one recipient unlocks the basic-card conversion.
exampleEn: During your Play Phase, you give one card to an ally, then give them a second card. You may now treat a card as a basic card. As lord, when you need Slash, you may ask other Shu characters whether they will play one for you.
misunderstandingsEn: Each recipient can receive Ren Virtue cards only if they have not received one earlier in that Play Phase.
The basic-card option occurs when you give the second Ren Virtue card during the phase.
Inspire is a lord skill and works only when Liu Bei is the lord.

skillsEn.textEn: During your Play Phase, you may give any number of hand cards to one other character who has not received a Ren Virtue card during this phase. When you give that character your second Ren Virtue card during this phase, you may treat a card as using a basic card.
skillsEn.plainEn: Give cards to an ally who has not received one this phase. The second card you give that ally lets you treat a card as a basic card.

skillsEn.textEn: Lord skill: When you need to use or play a Slash, you may ask other Shu characters to choose whether to play one Slash, treated as used or played by you.
skillsEn.plainEn: As lord, ask Shu allies to provide Slash for you.

summaryEn: Reimagined Guan Yu turns any red card into Slash and Diamonds Slash ignores range. Justice can either disable an enemy's skills and hand cards or take a red card and heal that character.
timingEn: Keep red cards for defense and conversion, and use Justice according to whether disabling an enemy or healing an ally is more valuable.
exampleEn: During your Play Phase, you discard a card to use Justice on an enemy. They reveal a black hand card, so their non-locked skills fail for the turn, they cannot use or play hand cards, and your Hearts Slash deals 1 extra damage to them.
misunderstandingsEn: Martial Saint converts any red card, not only a red basic card.
Only Diamonds Slash has unlimited range.
Justice's black-card effect lasts for the current turn only and does not disable locked skills.

skillsEn.textEn: You may use or play one red card as Slash. Diamonds Slash has no range limit when you use it.
skillsEn.plainEn: Any red card becomes Slash, and Diamonds Slash can reach anyone.

skillsEn.textEn: Once during your Play Phase, you may discard one card, then make one other character reveal one hand card. If it is black, that character's non-locked skills are ineffective for this turn, and they cannot use or play hand cards; damage dealt to them by your Hearts Slash is increased by 1. If it is red, you gain it, then you may make that character recover 1 HP.
skillsEn.plainEn: A black reveal shuts down non-locked skills, hand cards, and makes your Hearts Slash stronger. A red reveal gives you the card and may heal its owner.

summaryEn: Reimagined Zhang Fei can use unlimited Slash, and after the first Slash in his Play Phase his later Slash ignores distance. Substitute stores Slashes that target him without dealing damage.
timingEn: Use one Slash early to unlock unlimited range for the rest of the Play Phase, and preserve defense so Substitute can collect failed attacks.
exampleEn: You use Slash once during your Play Phase, so all later Slash you use this phase ignores distance. At the end of the phase, you activate Substitute, discard all your trick and mount cards, and until your next turn begins gain every Slash that targets you without dealing damage.
misunderstandingsEn: Unlimited Slash is a locked skill and does not require a weapon.
Distance is ignored only after you have used Slash during the current Play Phase.
Substitute discards all trick and mount cards before storing qualifying Slashes.

skillsEn.textEn: Locked skill: You may use Slash without limit. During your Play Phase, if you have used Slash during the current phase, Slash you use during this phase has no range limit.
skillsEn.plainEn: Use as many Slash as you want. After your first Slash, your later Slash this phase ignores distance.

skillsEn.textEn: At the end of your Play Phase, you may activate this skill. Discard all trick cards and mount cards you have. Then, until the start of your next turn, gain every Slash that chooses you as a target and does not deal damage to you.
skillsEn.plainEn: Clear away your tricks and mounts, then collect Slashes that fail to damage you until your next turn.

summaryEn: Reimagined Zhao Yun swaps Slash and Jink to maintain both offense and defense. Outside your turn, using or playing a hand card can reveal a top card for any character, with a discard cost if the categories differ.
timingEn: Use Dragon Courage to answer whichever basic card is needed, and trigger Cliff Edge outside your turn only when the revealed card's recipient is worth the possible discard.
exampleEn: Outside your turn, you play a hand card. Cliff Edge reveals the top card and gives it to an ally. If the revealed card's category differs from the card you played, you discard a card.
misunderstandingsEn: Dragon Courage converts Slash to Jink and Jink to Slash; it does not convert other cards.
Cliff Edge triggers outside your turn only.
The revealed card may be given to any character, but differing categories make you discard one card.

skillsEn.textEn: You may use or play one Slash as Jink, or one Jink as Slash.
skillsEn.plainEn: Swap Slash and Jink whenever you need the other card.

skillsEn.textEn: When you use or play a hand card outside your turn, you may reveal the top card of the draw pile and give it to any character. If the two cards have different categories, you discard one card.
skillsEn.plainEn: Outside your turn, reveal and give away the top card; pay one card if its category differs from the card you used.

summaryEn: Ma Chao attacks from farther away and can make his Slash very difficult to answer. Use Iron Cavalry against enemies who rely on non-locked defensive or damage-triggered skills.
timingEn: Use Iron Cavalry immediately after choosing a Slash target; its skill suppression lasts until the end of the current turn.
exampleEn: You use Slash on an enemy. You activate Iron Cavalry, making that enemy's non-locked skills ineffective for the turn, then perform judgment. If the result is spades and the enemy does not discard a spade card, the enemy cannot use Jink for this Slash.
misunderstandingsEn: Horse does not increase your attack range; it reduces your distance to other characters by 1.
Iron Cavalry is optional, but if activated, the target must discard a card matching the judgment suit or cannot use Jink for that Slash.
Iron Cavalry disables non-locked skills only; locked skills still work.

skillsEn.textEn: Locked skill: When you calculate your distance to other characters, reduce it by 1.
skillsEn.plainEn: You can reach other characters from one distance farther away. This does not increase your attack range.

skillsEn.textEn: When you use Slash to choose a target, you may make that target's non-locked skills ineffective until the end of this turn. Then you perform judgment. Unless that character discards a card whose suit matches the judgment result, that character cannot use Jink to respond to this Slash.
skillsEn.plainEn: You can temporarily shut off the target's non-locked skills. The target must also discard a card of the judgment suit or cannot play Jink against this Slash.

summaryEn: Gan Ning converts black cards into Sabotage to destroy enemy hand cards and equipment. His once-per-game skill can protect several characters from a multi-target trick or deny enemies its benefit.
timingEn: Use Raid when a black card can remove an important card. Save Ferocious Assault for the most impactful multi-target trick.
exampleEn: You use a black card as Sabotage on an enemy's equipment. Later, an enemy uses Barbarians on three characters; you activate Ferocious Assault to make Barbarians ineffective for two of those targets.
misunderstandingsEn: Raid converts any black card, not only a black basic card.
Ferocious Assault is limited to once per game.
Ferocious Assault can make the trick ineffective for any number of its targets, including allies or enemies.

skillsEn.textEn: You may use a black card as Sabotage.
skillsEn.plainEn: Any black card can become Sabotage. This lets you remove hand cards or equipment without needing an actual Sabotage.

skillsEn.textEn: Limited skill: After a trick card chooses two or more targets, you may make that card ineffective for any number of those target characters.
skillsEn.plainEn: Once per game, you can protect selected targets from a multi-target trick. The trick still affects targets you do not choose.

summaryEn: Lu Meng stores a large hand by avoiding Slash during his Play Phase, then awakens when his hand greatly exceeds his HP. After awakening, Mind's Eye lets him inspect and manipulate an enemy's heart cards.
timingEn: Decide before using Slash whether skipping your Discard Phase is more valuable than attacking. Awakening checks at the start of your preparation phase.
exampleEn: During your Play Phase, you use no Slash. At the end of the turn, you skip your Discard Phase. At a later preparation phase, with 7 hand cards and 4 HP, you meet the threshold and awaken, losing 1 maximum HP and gaining Mind's Eye.
misunderstandingsEn: You may skip the Discard Phase only if you did not use or play Slash during that Play Phase.
The awakening threshold is hand cards at least 3 higher than HP, or at least 2 higher in games with 7 or more players.
Mind's Eye can reveal only another character's hand and can affect only a revealed Heart card.

skillsEn.textEn: If you have not used or played Slash during your Play Phase, you may skip your Discard Phase.
skillsEn.plainEn: Avoid Slash during your Play Phase to keep all your cards without discarding down to your hand limit.

skillsEn.textEn: Awakening skill: During your preparation phase, if your number of hand cards is at least 3 greater than your HP (or at least 2 greater in a game with 7 or more players), you lose 1 maximum HP and gain Mind's Eye. Once during your Play Phase, you may view another character's hand, then you may reveal one Heart card from it and choose one: 1. discard that card; or 2. place that card on top of the draw pile.
skillsEn.plainEn: Build a large hand to awaken. Afterward, once each Play Phase, you can inspect an enemy's hand and discard or stack a revealed Heart card.

summaryEn: Huang Gai loses HP to draw cards, then gains powerful red Slashes during his Play Phase. Use this to prepare a burst with extra Slash capacity and Slashes that cannot be answered by Jink.
timingEn: Use Bitter Resolve before your key attacks when losing 1 HP will trigger Surrender and improve your damage output.
exampleEn: During your Play Phase, you discard a card with Bitter Resolve and lose 1 HP. Surrender makes you draw three cards; because this is your Play Phase, your red Slashes ignore distance, cannot be answered by Jink, and you may use one additional Slash until the turn ends.
misunderstandingsEn: Bitter Resolve can be used only once during your Play Phase and requires discarding a card.
Surrender is mandatory after you lose 1 HP: you draw three cards.
The enhanced red Slash effects last only until the end of the current turn and apply when you are the user.

skillsEn.textEn: Once during your Play Phase, you may discard one card, then lose 1 HP.
skillsEn.plainEn: Trade one card and 1 HP for the chance to trigger Surrender. The HP loss is not damage.

skillsEn.textEn: Locked skill: After you lose 1 HP, you draw three cards. If you are currently in your Play Phase, then until the end of the turn, your red Slashes have no distance restriction, cannot be responded to with Jink, and you may use one additional Slash.
skillsEn.plainEn: Every 1 HP lost gives you three cards. Losing HP during your Play Phase also supercharges your red Slashes for that turn.

summaryEn: Zhou Yu draws extra cards and keeps a hand limit equal to his maximum HP. His Interrogation forces another character to choose between revealing and losing cards of one suit or losing HP.
timingEn: Use Interrogation when the target is likely to hold several cards of the handed card's suit or cannot afford to lose 1 HP.
exampleEn: During your Play Phase, you give an enemy a face-up red card. The enemy chooses to reveal all hand cards and discard all cards matching that card's suit instead of losing 1 HP.
misunderstandingsEn: Eloquent Hero gives one extra card during the Draw Phase, not during every draw.
Your hand limit is your maximum HP, not your current HP.
The target chooses the Interrogation outcome after receiving the face-up card.

skillsEn.textEn: Locked skill: During your Draw Phase, you draw one additional card. Your hand limit equals X, where X is your maximum HP.
skillsEn.plainEn: You draw one extra card every Draw Phase. Your maximum hand size is based on maximum HP.

skillsEn.textEn: Once during your Play Phase, you may give one hand card face up to another character. That character chooses one: 1. reveal all hand cards, then discard all cards whose suit matches the given card; or 2. lose 1 HP.
skillsEn.plainEn: Give an enemy a card and make them choose between exposing and losing cards of that suit or losing HP.

summaryEn: Da Qiao uses Diamond cards to apply or remove Indulgence and draws a card afterward. She can also redirect Slash away from herself to another character in her attack range.
timingEn: Use National Beauty either before an enemy's important turn or to remove an ally's Indulgence. Keep a discardable card ready when you expect to be targeted by Slash.
exampleEn: During your Play Phase, you use a Diamond card as Indulgence on an enemy and draw a card. Later, another character uses Slash on you; you discard a card and transfer that Slash to an enemy within your attack range.
misunderstandingsEn: National Beauty is limited to once during your Play Phase, but its two options are choices of the same activation.
The second option removes an Indulgence already on the field; it does not cancel any delayed trick.
Flowing Away requires a discard and a different character within your attack range.

skillsEn.textEn: Once during your Play Phase, choose one: 1. use a Diamond card as Indulgence; or 2. discard a Diamond card and discard one Indulgence on the field. After completing your choice, draw one card.
skillsEn.plainEn: Diamond cards can place Indulgence or remove one already in play. Either option lets you draw a card.

skillsEn.textEn: When you become the target of Slash, you may discard one card and choose another character within your attack range. Transfer this Slash to that character.
skillsEn.plainEn: You can discard a card to redirect Slash to someone you can attack. The new target must be another character.

summaryEn: Lu Xun can hide his entire hand from a single-target delayed trick or ordinary trick, then recover it at turn end. Emptying his hand can also let several characters draw through Connected Camp.
timingEn: Use Humility only when protecting the whole hand is worth losing access to it until the turn ends. Plan how many cards to discard so Connected Camp can benefit the right allies.
exampleEn: An enemy uses a single-target Sabotage on you. You activate Humility and place all your hand cards on your general card; at the end of the turn, you take them back. If you later lose three hand cards and have none left, Connected Camp can make up to three characters draw one card each.
misunderstandingsEn: Humility works only when the delayed trick or another character's ordinary trick has you as its sole target.
Cards placed by Humility return at the end of the current turn, not immediately.
Connected Camp may affect at most as many characters as the number of hand cards lost in that event.

skillsEn.textEn: When a delayed trick or an ordinary trick used by another character takes effect on you, if you are the only target of that card, you may place all your hand cards face down on your general card. At the end of this turn, you gain those cards.
skillsEn.plainEn: You can hide your entire hand from a single-target trick and retrieve it at the end of the turn.

skillsEn.textEn: When you lose hand cards, if you have no hand cards afterward, you may choose up to X characters to each draw one card, where X is the number of hand cards lost in this event.
skillsEn.plainEn: If losing cards empties your hand, you can let several characters draw. The number of possible targets equals the number of cards you lost.

summaryEn: Cao Cao turns damage into cards and can reclaim the damaging card for later use. As lord, he can ask other Wei characters to provide Jink for him.
timingEn: Accept damage only when the cards gained are worth it and you can survive. As lord, use Imperial Protection when you need Jink and Wei allies can respond.
exampleEn: An enemy uses Barbarians to deal you damage. After the damage, you use Treacherous Hero to gain Barbarians and draw one card. If you are the lord and later need Jink, you may ask other Wei characters to choose whether to play Jink for you.
misunderstandingsEn: Treacherous Hero is optional; you do not automatically gain every damaging card.
You gain the card that caused the damage, not necessarily a card from the damage source's hand.
Imperial Protection works only when you are the lord and you need to use or play Jink.

skillsEn.textEn: After you take damage, you may gain the card that caused the damage and draw one card.
skillsEn.plainEn: When damage hits you, you can take the damaging card and draw one more card.

skillsEn.textEn: Lord skill: When you need to use or play Jink, you may ask other Wei characters to choose whether to play one Jink, treated as being used or played by you.
skillsEn.plainEn: As lord, you can ask Wei allies to defend you with their Jink. Each ally chooses whether to help.

summaryEn: Sima Yi can steal a card from a source that damages him and can replace judgment cards before they take effect. Save suitable cards to prevent harmful judgments or force Lightning toward an enemy.
timingEn: Keep a replacement card available before important judgments resolve. Use Feedback after damage when the source has a valuable card.
exampleEn: You take 1 damage from an enemy and activate Feedback to gain one card from that enemy. Before an ally's Indulgence judgment takes effect, you play a different card to replace its judgment card.
misunderstandingsEn: Feedback is optional and triggers after 1 damage is dealt to you.
Feedback gains one card from the damage source's area; it does not necessarily take a hand card.
Ghostly Wisdom replaces the judgment card before it takes effect, not after the result is applied.

skillsEn.textEn: After you take 1 damage, you may gain one card from the damage source's area.
skillsEn.plainEn: When someone damages you, you can take one of that character's cards.

skillsEn.textEn: Before a character's judgment card takes effect, you may play one card to replace it.
skillsEn.plainEn: You can change a judgment result before it resolves by playing a replacement card.

summaryEn: Xiahou Dun punishes damage with either damage or card removal through Ferocious Assault. He can store cards gained outside the Draw Phase and give them to other characters after a turn ends.
timingEn: Use Ferocious Assault after damage when the judgment color gives the better punishment. Store cards with Frugality when an ally will need them after the current turn.
exampleEn: You take 1 damage and activate Ferocious Assault. The judgment is black, so you discard one card from the damage source. Later, you gain cards outside your Draw Phase, store some with Frugality, and after a character's turn ends give them to an ally.
misunderstandingsEn: Ferocious Assault's red result damages the source; its black result discards one of the source's cards.
Frugality triggers only for cards gained outside the Draw Phase.
Stored cards are given after a character's turn ends, not immediately when they are stored.

skillsEn.textEn: After you take 1 damage, you may perform judgment. If the result is red, you deal 1 damage to the damage source; if it is black, you discard one card from that character.
skillsEn.plainEn: After damage, a red judgment hurts the source and a black judgment removes one of the source's cards.

skillsEn.textEn: After you gain cards outside your Draw Phase, you may place any number of those cards face down on your general card. After a character's turn ends, you give those cards to other characters.
skillsEn.plainEn: You can save cards gained outside your Draw Phase. After a turn ends, you give the saved cards to other characters.

summaryEn: Zhang Liao gives up some Draw Phase cards to take the same number of hand cards from other characters. Target enemies with valuable cards or weak hands that contain only survival cards.
timingEn: At the start of your Draw Phase, decide how many cards to forgo and which characters are worth targeting.
exampleEn: During your Draw Phase, you choose to draw one fewer card. You then take one hand card from each of two other characters.
misunderstandingsEn: You may take at most one card from each chosen other character.
You must give up the same number of cards you would otherwise draw.
Raid is optional; you may simply draw normally.

skillsEn.textEn: During your Draw Phase, you may draw any number of cards fewer than normal and gain one hand card from each of the same number of other characters.
skillsEn.plainEn: Skip some of your normal draws to steal one hand card from each of that many other characters.

summaryEn: Xu Chu reveals the top three cards to obtain basic cards, weapons, and Duel, trading away his normal Draw Phase. If he does, his Slash and Duel damage increases until his next turn begins.
timingEn: Use Bare-Chested when the revealed cards improve your attack and you can exploit the temporary damage bonus.
exampleEn: At the start of your Draw Phase, you reveal three cards and gain the basic cards, weapon cards, and Duel among them. You skip drawing for the phase, and your Slash and Duel damage as the source are increased by 1 until your next turn begins.
misunderstandingsEn: Bare-Chested does not automatically give you all three revealed cards; it gives only basic cards, weapon cards, and Duel.
If you choose to gain those cards, you skip the Draw Phase.
The damage bonus lasts until your next turn starts, not merely until the end of the current turn.

skillsEn.textEn: At the start of your Draw Phase, reveal the top three cards of the draw pile. You may gain the basic cards, weapon cards, and Duel among them. If you do, you skip your Draw Phase, and until the start of your next turn, Slash and Duel for which you are the source deal 1 additional damage.
skillsEn.plainEn: Reveal three cards and take the useful attack cards, but give up your normal draw. In exchange, your Slash and Duel deal +1 damage until your next turn.

summaryEn: Guo Jia gains cards after taking damage and can distribute up to two of them to allies. He can also reclaim judgment cards after they resolve, turning judgment manipulation into card advantage.
timingEn: Stay alive while accepting worthwhile damage, then use Last Words to place key cards with the characters who need them.
exampleEn: You take 1 damage and activate Last Words, drawing two cards. You give one card to an ally and keep the other. Later, after your judgment card takes effect, Heaven's Insight lets you gain that judgment card.
misunderstandingsEn: Last Words lets you give up to two cards, not necessarily all the cards you draw.
The recipients must be one or two other characters.
Heaven's Insight triggers after your judgment card takes effect.

skillsEn.textEn: After your judgment card takes effect, you may gain that card.
skillsEn.plainEn: You can take your own resolved judgment card into your hand.

skillsEn.textEn: After you take 1 damage, you may draw two cards. Then you may give up to two hand cards to one or two other characters.
skillsEn.plainEn: Damage lets you draw two cards and optionally pass up to two cards to allies.

summaryEn: Li Dian rearranges the top four cards to choose the best two for his next draws. Mutual drawing after damage helps him maintain resources and compensates characters affected by damage.
timingEn: Use Observant Scholar at the start of the Draw Phase to keep cards suited to the current situation. Trigger Mutual Exertion after damage when both characters benefit from a card.
exampleEn: At the start of your Draw Phase, you view the top four cards, place two on top in your chosen order, and place the other two on the bottom. You then draw from the arranged top cards.
misunderstandingsEn: Observant Scholar does not draw the viewed cards immediately; it only arranges them.
You must place exactly two of the four cards on top and the rest on the bottom.
Mutual Exertion triggers after either dealing 1 damage to another character or taking 1 damage from another character.

skillsEn.textEn: At the start of your Draw Phase, you may view the top four cards of the draw pile. Then place two of them on top of the draw pile and the remaining cards on the bottom of the draw pile.
skillsEn.plainEn: Look at the next four cards and choose which two you will draw first. The other two go to the bottom.

skillsEn.textEn: After you deal 1 damage to another character, or after you take 1 damage dealt by another character, you may have you and that character each draw one card.
skillsEn.plainEn: After damage between you and another character, both of you may draw one card.

summaryEn: Hua Tuo turns red cards into Peaches outside his turn and can heal several different characters during his Play Phase. Red discards let Green Pouch continue, but each repeated use must choose a character not previously chosen that turn.
timingEn: Save red cards for emergency Peaches outside your turn, and use Green Pouch repeatedly only when different characters need healing.
exampleEn: Outside your turn, you use a red card as Peach on a dying ally. During your Play Phase, you discard a red card to heal one character, then activate Green Pouch again to discard another card and heal a different character.
misunderstandingsEn: Emergency Aid works only outside your turn.
Green Pouch is initially once per Play Phase, but a red discarded card allows another activation.
Repeated Green Pouch activations cannot choose a character already chosen by this skill during the current turn.

skillsEn.textEn: Outside your turn, you may use a red card as Peach.
skillsEn.plainEn: On other characters' turns, any red card can become Peach. This can save a dying character.

skillsEn.textEn: Once during your Play Phase, you may discard one hand card to have one character recover 1 HP. If the discarded card is red, you may activate this skill again, but you cannot choose a character chosen by this skill earlier this turn.
skillsEn.plainEn: Discard a hand card to heal someone. Red discards let you heal again, but each use must target a different character.

summaryEn: Lu Bu's Slash requires two Jinks and his Duels demand two Slashes per response, making his attacks difficult to answer. After a damaging Slash, he can take a card and either draw or force another Duel.
timingEn: Use Slash against characters with limited defense, and choose the equipment option of Mastery of Arms only when the resulting Duel is advantageous.
exampleEn: Your Slash deals damage to another character. You gain one card from that character's area. If it is not equipment, you draw one card; if it is equipment, you are treated as using Duel against another character chosen by that character.
misunderstandingsEn: A target needs two Jinks to cancel Lu Bu's Slash, not one.
During a Duel with Lu Bu, each response requires two Slashes.
Mastery of Arms can take a card from any of the target's areas, not only the hand.

skillsEn.textEn: Locked skill: Each Slash you use requires two Jinks to be canceled. A character in a Duel with you must play two Slashes each time they respond.
skillsEn.plainEn: Your Slash needs two Jinks to stop. Opponents in your Duels must play two Slashes for every response.

skillsEn.textEn: After your Slash deals damage to another character, you may gain one card from that character's area. If the card gained is not equipment, that character draws one card; if it is equipment, you are treated as using Duel against another character chosen by that character.
skillsEn.plainEn: A damaging Slash lets you take one card. Non-equipment gives the target a replacement draw; equipment makes you use Duel against a character the target chooses.

summaryEn: Gongsun Zan's distance changes with his HP, giving him reach when healthy and protection when wounded. Black Slashes can remove enemy equipment and let him claim a discarded mount.
timingEn: Use Black Slash against equipped enemies. Manage your HP carefully because it switches between offensive and defensive distance benefits.
exampleEn: You use a black Slash to deal damage to an enemy, then activate Fierce Valor to discard an equipment card from that enemy. When that card enters the discard pile and is a mount, you gain it.
misunderstandingsEn: Fierce Valor first discards the equipment; you gain it only if it enters the discard pile and is a mount.
When you have more than 2 HP, your distance to others is reduced by 1.
When you have 2 HP or less, other characters' distance to you increases by 1; this does not reduce your distance to them.

skillsEn.textEn: After a black Slash you use deals damage to a character, you may discard one card from that character's equipment area. Then, when that card is placed into the discard pile, if it is a mount card, you gain it.
skillsEn.plainEn: A damaging black Slash can destroy enemy equipment. If the discarded equipment is a mount, you take it after it reaches the discard pile.

skillsEn.textEn: Locked skill: If your HP is greater than 2, reduce your distance to other characters by 1. If your HP is no greater than 2, increase other characters' distance to you by 1.
skillsEn.plainEn: Above 2 HP, you can reach farther. At 2 HP or less, others have more difficulty reaching you.

summaryEn: Huang Yueying draws from using ordinary tricks and can convert basic cards into temporary hand-limit increases. Her locked skill removes distance restrictions from tricks and protects her defensive equipment and treasures from being discarded by others.
timingEn: Chain ordinary tricks to build cards and hand capacity, while keeping basic cards available for the temporary hand-limit increase.
exampleEn: You use an ordinary trick and activate Wisdom, drawing a card. The drawn card is a basic card, so you discard it and increase your hand limit by 1 for the turn. Your trick ignores distance, and another character cannot discard your armor or treasure.
misunderstandingsEn: Wisdom triggers when you use an ordinary trick, not every trick card.
The extra hand-limit effect requires the drawn card to be basic and lasts only this turn.
Ingenuity protects armor and treasure from other characters discarding them; it does not protect every equipment card.

skillsEn.textEn: When you use an ordinary trick card, you may draw one card. If that card is a basic card, you may discard it to increase your hand limit by 1 until the end of this turn.
skillsEn.plainEn: Using an ordinary trick can draw a card. If the drawn card is basic, you may discard it for +1 hand limit this turn.

skillsEn.textEn: Locked skill: The trick cards you use have no distance restriction. Other characters cannot discard armor or treasure cards from your equipment area.
skillsEn.plainEn: Your trick cards can reach any legal target regardless of distance. Others cannot discard your armor or treasure.

summaryEn: Zhuge Liang controls the top of the draw pile to improve his own draws and influence judgments. Emptying his hand makes him an invalid target for Slash and Duel, but do not discard recklessly just to activate that defense.
timingEn: Use Star Gazing during preparation to set up your next cards and judgments. Put all viewed cards on the bottom only when a second Star Gazing in the Finish Phase is worthwhile.
exampleEn: During your preparation phase, you view the top five cards, place two on top in a chosen order, and put three on the bottom. Later, if you have no hand cards, Locked Fort cannot be targeted by Slash or Duel.
misunderstandingsEn: Star Gazing views five cards, or three when fewer than four players are alive.
The extra Finish Phase Star Gazing is available only if all viewed cards were placed on the bottom.
Locked Fort prevents being chosen as a target; it does not cancel a Slash or Duel that already targeted you.

skillsEn.textEn: During your preparation phase, you may view the top five cards of the draw pile, or three cards if fewer than four players are alive. Then place them back on top or bottom of the draw pile in any order. If you place all of those cards on the bottom, you may perform Star Gazing again during your Finish Phase.
skillsEn.plainEn: Arrange the top cards to set up future draws and judgments. Sending all of them to the bottom lets you look again at the end of the turn.

skillsEn.textEn: Locked skill: If you have no hand cards, you cannot be chosen as a target of Slash or Duel.
skillsEn.plainEn: With an empty hand, Slash and Duel cannot target you. This does not protect you from other effects.

summaryEn: Sun Quan exchanges unwanted cards for new ones and gains an extra card when he completely empties his hand. As lord, healthy Wu allies can turn their Peaches into healing for him and draw afterward.
timingEn: Use Balance to seek needed cards without throwing away too many useful ones. As lord, rely on healthy Wu allies when your HP is low.
exampleEn: During your Play Phase, you discard all three hand cards with Balance and draw four cards. As lord, a Wu ally with higher HP uses Peach on themself, changes it to heal you for 1 HP, and then draws a card.
misunderstandingsEn: Balance is limited to once during your Play Phase.
The extra card requires discarding all your hand cards, not merely some of them.
Rescue works only when Sun Quan is lord and the Wu character has higher HP than him.

skillsEn.textEn: Once during your Play Phase, you may discard any number of cards, then draw the same number of cards. If you discarded all your hand cards this way, draw one additional card.
skillsEn.plainEn: Exchange any number of cards for the same number of new cards. Emptying your hand gives one extra draw.

skillsEn.textEn: Lord skill: When another Wu character uses Peach on themself, if that character's HP is greater than yours, they may instead make you recover 1 HP, then they draw one card.
skillsEn.plainEn: As lord, a healthier Wu ally can redirect their Peach to heal you and draw a card.

summaryEn: Sun Shangxiang heals herself or a male character while rewarding the healthier of the two with a card. Losing equipment lets her draw two cards, so equipment changes can fuel her hand.
timingEn: Use Marriage when either you or a male ally needs healing, and plan equipment replacement to trigger Phoenix Spirit.
exampleEn: During your Play Phase, you choose a male ally and discard a hand card. You have lower HP, so you recover 1 HP, while the ally has higher HP and draws one card. Later, when you lose an equipment card, Phoenix Spirit lets you draw two cards.
misunderstandingsEn: Marriage can choose only a male character.
You must either discard one hand card or place an equipment card into that character's equipment area.
The higher-HP character draws; the lower-HP character recovers 1 HP.

skillsEn.textEn: Once during your Play Phase, choose one male character and either discard one hand card or place one equipment card into that character's equipment area. The character among you and that character with higher HP draws one card; the character with lower HP recovers 1 HP.
skillsEn.plainEn: Choose a male character and pay a card. The healthier of you draws, while the one with lower HP heals.

skillsEn.textEn: When you lose one card from your equipment area, you may draw two cards.
skillsEn.plainEn: Losing equipment can draw two cards. This includes equipment leaving your equipment area.

summaryEn: Zhen Ji repeatedly judges for black cards and keeps the cards gained this way outside the current hand limit. She can also turn any black hand card into Jink.
timingEn: Use Goddess Luo at preparation when you want extra cards for the turn, and preserve black cards so Allure can provide defense.
exampleEn: During your preparation phase, you judge black and gain the judgment card, then judge again and gain another black card. Those gained cards do not count toward your hand limit this turn; you can also use a black hand card as Jink later.
misunderstandingsEn: Goddess Luo stops when a judgment is not black.
Cards gained by Goddess Luo are not counted against your hand limit for the current turn only.
Allure converts black hand cards into Jink for both using and playing Jink.

skillsEn.textEn: During your preparation phase, you may perform judgment. If the result is black, you gain that card and may repeat this process. Cards gained this way do not count toward your hand limit this turn.
skillsEn.plainEn: Keep judging while the result is black, taking each black judgment card. These cards are exempt from your hand limit for this turn.

skillsEn.textEn: You may use or play a black hand card as Jink.
skillsEn.plainEn: Any black card in your hand can become Jink when you need to use or play one.

summaryEn: Diao Chan makes two other male characters fight each other with Duel. Her Finish Phase draw becomes two cards when she has no hand cards, helping her maintain defense.
timingEn: Use Lure when the chosen male characters have mismatched Slash resources, and use it to make an enemy attack another enemy or an ally finish an enemy.
exampleEn: During your Play Phase, you discard a card and choose two male enemies. You make one of them be treated as using Duel on the other. At the end of your turn, because you have no hand cards, Closed Moon lets you draw two cards.
misunderstandingsEn: Lure is limited to once during your Play Phase and requires a discard.
Both chosen characters must be other male characters.
Closed Moon draws two cards only when you have no hand cards at the moment it resolves.

skillsEn.textEn: Once during your Play Phase, you may discard one card and choose two other male characters. One of those male characters is treated as using Duel on the other.
skillsEn.plainEn: Discard a card to make two other male characters Duel. You choose which one starts the Duel.

skillsEn.textEn: During your Finish Phase, you may draw one card. If you have no hand cards, draw two cards instead.
skillsEn.plainEn: Draw at the end of your turn. Having no hand cards doubles this draw.
